scope: TileLang DSv4 sparse-MQA backward diagnostic that restores dKV contribution math while skipping global dKV writeback.

read: TileLang full-shape QK, softmax-backward, dQ, dAttnSink, and dKV contribution GEMMs fit and run in 2.27 ms when global dKV writes are removed.
The dKV contribution GEMMs add only about 0.09 ms over the no-dKV diagnostic, so the producer math is not the bottleneck in this schedule.
The straight reference remains slow because its per-token CTA emits per-selected T.atomic_addx4 dKV writes and must split heads at full width.
next_kernel_contract: Do not keep sweeping the straight reference.
Start from the direct H64/D512 TileLang schedule for QK/softmax-backward/dQ.

Rewrite only the dKV write path: either extend the producer to own a token window with tile-local segmented accumulation by compressed group, or emit compact keyed partial rows for a small final reducer.
A same-token-only reducer is not enough because the current CTA owns one token and duplicate compressed groups inside one token's selected block are rare.
decision.promotion_gate: At S4096/H64/D512/topk512, the full dQ+dKV backward candidate must beat the 8.01 ms current Triton sparse-atomic dKV wall; a useful first TileLang target is dQ/no-write 2.19 ms plus reduced dKV writeback under roughly 5.7 ms.
